title: YOLO Release Stability Baseline
description: Turn the Trello dogfood failures into YOLO product fixes: discovery input fidelity, PRD/check strictness, runner/provider stability, controlled recovery, and release gates that block publishable claims until a deterministic full lifecycle passes without manual agent takeover.

approval.reason: The user requested a publishable YOLO product PRD based on YOLO failures exposed by the Trello dogfood run, not more Trello application work.

question_trace.question: What did the Trello dogfood expose about YOLO itself?
question_trace.answer: YOLO could not hold one lifecycle baseline across discovery, PRD, check, runner, review, acceptance, and release evidence.

question_trace.question: What outcome is required?
question_trace.answer: A publishable YOLO software baseline that is runner-stable and fail-closed, with no late-stage manual agent takeover counted as product success.

prd_intake.problem: YOLO currently has strong local modules and tests, but the dogfood run showed gaps between module evidence and end-to-end product reliability.
success_criteria: Discovery preserves structured user intent from markdown and file inputs.
Executable PRDs fail check when demand, atomicity, adapter, or evidence contracts are weak.
Runner/provider failures produce terminal evidence instead of hanging, silently retrying, or relying on manual agent takeover.
Release readiness requires deterministic full lifecycle dogfood evidence before public-use claims.
non_goals: Do not continue Trello feature implementation in this PRD.
Do not publish, read credentials, or execute billable providers.
Do not claim absolute stability; define deterministic gates and residual manual release decisions.

target_files.evidence: Documents the YOLO lifecycle and run gating.
Documents demand, atomicity, session handoff, and readiness goals.
Documents release-side deliverables and dogfood expectations.
Defines PRD v2 contract, demand readiness, task scope, and supported post conditions.
Current check report treats some execution-unsafe states as warnings.
Current contract doctor allows target_file_modified to satisfy executable target coverage.
Builds real provider invocations and handles timeout/preflight output.
Creates git and filesystem worktrees and writes baselines.
Records private package, 0.1.0 version, experimental runtime boundary, and latest full test count.

assumptions.statement: The current package is not yet public-release ready because private=true, runtime API stability, external release, and public dogfood evidence remain blockers.
This PRD should create release-stabilization work for YOLO itself, not modify the Trello target project.

checks.message: The PRD explicitly uses Trello dogfood as evidence for YOLO product failures.
Tasks are ordered by subsystem and target one gate or runtime failure mode at a time.
Every task has executable test/build/code post conditions beyond manual acceptance.
Each task is intended for one fresh session with explicit scope, evidence, and dependencies.

next_actions: Run yolo check on this PRD, then execute tasks in dependency order with deterministic tests before any release claim.

execution_readiness.expected_task_session: one fresh runner session per task; no shared hidden state and no manual agent takeover counted as success

requirements.text: YOLO discovery must preserve structured intent from markdown headings and explicit file inputs instead of treating a path or raw markdown format as the requirement itself.

demand_trace.evidence: Trello dogfood exposed markdown/file intake drift.

requirements.text: YOLO check/preflight must fail closed for executable PRDs with missing demand contracts, investigation-first atomicity warnings, weak post-condition gates, or unresolved project facts.

demand_trace.evidence: Existing checks surfaced DEMAND_CONTRACT_MISSING and ATOMICITY_INVESTIGATE_FIRST as warnings during an execution-intended flow.

requirements.text: Acceptance adapters must be versioned against the requested surface and scenario so stale MVP adapters cannot validate a later V2/V3 product target.

demand_trace.evidence: Trello acceptance drift showed adapter pass can be disconnected from target product scope.

requirements.text: Provider execution must expose the exact command, cwd, stdin, budget, sandbox, timeout, output capture, and terminal result before the runner can mark a task complete.

demand_trace.evidence: Provider sessions hung or partially wrote output without producing a reliable lifecycle completion record.

requirements.text: Runner worktrees must be dependency-stable for git and filesystem modes, and stale runner state must never block a new run after the old process is gone.

demand_trace.evidence: Dogfood logs exposed stale runner.pid and isolated worktree dependency failures such as missing Vite.

requirements.text: Controlled recovery may import verified evidence, but it must preserve that the original runner path failed and may only unlock review/accept/ship through an explicit recovery ledger.

demand_trace.evidence: Trello passed independent checks but YOLO lifecycle remained blocked because no clean run evidence existed.

requirements.text: Public-use or publishable claims must be blocked until a deterministic fake-provider full lifecycle fixture passes from init/discovery/PRD/check/run/review/accept/ship with no manual agent takeover counted as success.

demand_trace.evidence: Current release docs show strong module coverage but still lack real public dogfood and billable execution evidence.

designs.text: Discovery normalization supports markdown section headings, colon labels, and explicit requirement-file input, with tests that prove target_users/problem/scope/success_criteria survive the path-to-artifact boundary.
Check and PRD contract doctor promote execution-unsafe warnings to blockers for L3 AFK-ready PRDs and reject tasks whose only executable gate is a file-change signal.
Adapter readiness compares PRD surface/scenario/version expectations with the selected acceptance adapter manifest before UI or product acceptance can pass.

Provider and runner logs become a signed invocation/result ledger: dry-run matrix proves the command contract, real runs record exact invocation evidence, and timeout/empty-output/partial-output failures become terminal or retry-limited outcomes.
Controlled recovery has a separate evidence import path that can unlock later gates only when artifacts, commands, timestamps, target files, and post-condition results are verified.

Release readiness includes a deterministic fake-provider fixture that exercises the full lifecycle and fails closed when any segment relies on manual code takeover, missing runner evidence, or no-code-only dogfood.

state_matrix.discovery: Markdown and file inputs produce the same structured discovery brief as equivalent CLI fields.
state_matrix.check: Executable PRDs with missing demand, weak gates, stale adapter, or investigate-first tasks are blocked before run.
state_matrix.run: Provider invocation evidence is recorded before execution and terminal failures cannot masquerade as successful implementation.
state_matrix.recovery: Verified external evidence can be imported only through controlled recovery and is visibly distinct from a clean YOLO run.
state_matrix.release: Release gates stay blocked until deterministic full lifecycle dogfood evidence exists.

success_metrics.primary: A fake-provider stabilization fixture can complete init -> discovery -> plan -> PRD -> check -> run -> review -> accept -> ship without manual agent takeover.
secondary: yolo check blocks weak executable PRDs before provider spawn.
provider timeout, empty output, and partial output all produce durable terminal evidence.
release readiness text and doctor output stop claiming publishable readiness while private/runtime/public-dogfood blockers remain.

out_of_scope: No npm publish, credential reads, or billable provider calls.
No runtime public API stable-boundary promotion without explicit release decision evidence.
No broad refactor of runner internals beyond the scoped gate/runtime fixes below.

tasks.title: Parse markdown discovery headings
tasks.description: Make discovery readiness treat common markdown headings such as '## Target User', '## Problem', '## Scope', and '## Success Criteria' as structured fields, not opaque prose.

targets.description: Discovery label extraction and readiness normalization

acceptance_criteria: A markdown brief with section headings yields target_users, problem, scope, and success_criteria in the discovery brief.
Existing colon-label and explicit field behavior remains unchanged.
tasks.verification_hint: Run the focused discovery gate test and inspect the new markdown-heading fixture.

post_conditions.message: Discovery gate implementation must change.
Discovery gate tests must include the markdown-heading regression.
The regression test must cover markdown target-user headings.
Discovery gate tests must pass.

tasks.title: Read explicit discovery files
tasks.description: Ensure yolo discover treats an explicit markdown requirement file as input content instead of treating the file path string as the user's requirement.

targets.description: discover argument parsing and file-path forwarding

acceptance_criteria: Passing a requirement markdown file produces the same discovery artifact as passing its text.
The generated discovery artifact does not place the raw file path into active requirements.
tasks.verification_hint: Use a temp markdown file in the focused discovery runtime test.

post_conditions.message: CLI discover parsing must change.
CLI must forward explicit requirement files into discovery runtime input.
Discovery runtime and gate tests must pass after CLI forwarding changes.

tasks.title: Normalize discovery file content
tasks.description: Normalize explicit requirement file content inside discovery runtime so generated artifacts use the file body, not the file path string.
acceptance_criteria: Discovery runtime reads requirementFile or inputFile content before readiness analysis.
The generated discovery artifact does not place the raw file path into active requirements.
tasks.verification_hint: Use a temp markdown file in the focused discovery runtime test.

post_conditions.message: Discovery runtime normalization must change.
Discovery runtime tests must include explicit file input.
The regression test must exercise requirementFile input.
Discovery runtime and gate tests must pass.

tasks.title: Block executable PRDs without demand
tasks.description: Make yolo check and PRD preflight block new execution-intended PRDs that lack approved demand readiness instead of allowing DEMAND_CONTRACT_MISSING as a warning.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy planning-only PRDs may still produce advisory demand warnings.
L3/executable/default-run PRDs without approved demand are blocked before provider execution.
tasks.verification_hint: The focused check-report suite must assert blocked status for an execution-intended missing-demand PRD.

post_conditions.message: Check report demand policy must change.
Check report tests must cover the missing-demand blocker.
Tests must assert DEMAND_CONTRACT_MISSING behavior.
Check report tests must pass.

tasks.title: Block investigate-first execution
tasks.description: Promote ATOMICITY_INVESTIGATE_FIRST from warning to blocker for L3 AFK-ready execution PRDs unless the task is explicitly planning_only or investigation_only.
acceptance_criteria: Execution-intended PRDs are blocked when a task requires investigation before patching.
Planning-only or investigation-only artifacts can still report the issue without pretending they are runnable.
tasks.verification_hint: The check-report suite must include an AFK-ready PRD with an investigate-first task and expect blocked status.

post_conditions.message: Check report atomicity policy must change.
Check report tests must include atomicity blocker coverage.
Tests must assert ATOMICITY_INVESTIGATE_FIRST behavior.
Check report and story atomicity tests must pass.

tasks.title: Reject weak-only task gates
tasks.description: Update PRD contract doctor so pending executable tasks cannot pass contract checks when their only executable FAIL post conditions are target_file_modified/file_exists-style presence gates or manual acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: A task with only target_file_modified plus acceptance_criteria is blocked before runner execution.
A task with target coverage plus tests_pass, test_file_passes, build_pass, code_contains, or code_matches remains executable.
tasks.verification_hint: Run the PRD contract doctor focused suite and inspect the new weak-only gate fixture.

post_conditions.message: PRD contract doctor must change.
Contract doctor tests must cover weak-only gates.
Contract doctor must emit a stable weak-gate blocker code.
PRD contract doctor tests must pass.

tasks.title: Version acceptance adapter evidence
tasks.description: Extend adapter evidence collection so the selected acceptance adapter records its adapter id, surface id, scenario id, contract version, and compatibility result in evidence artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter evidence contains stable adapter identity and surface/scenario contract metadata.
Adapter evidence reports incompatible or missing version data as blocked, not pass.
tasks.verification_hint: The adapter evidence collector focused suite should assert compatibility metadata in the output payload.

post_conditions.message: Adapter evidence collector must change.
Adapter evidence collector tests must change.
Adapter evidence must include compatibility metadata.
Adapter evidence collector tests must pass.

tasks.title: Block stale adapter selection
tasks.description: Make check readiness compare the PRD's requested product surface contract with the selected acceptance adapter and block stale or unknown adapter compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: A product PRD with a mismatched adapter contract is blocked before run or acceptance.
Resolver unknown/custom adapters remain blocked for product acceptance unless a compatible manifest is present.
tasks.verification_hint: Run check-report tests with a fixture that asks for a V3 surface while the manifest declares an MVP/V1 surface.

post_conditions.message: Readiness policy must include adapter compatibility logic.
Readiness policy must expose stale adapter mismatch blocking.
Adapter readiness policy and evidence tests must pass.

tasks.title: Report stale adapter blockers
tasks.description: Wire adapter compatibility blockers from readiness policy into check report output for execution-intended PRDs.
acceptance_criteria: Check report blocks a product PRD whose selected adapter contract is stale.
The report includes a stable ADAPTER_CONTRACT_MISMATCH blocker code.
tasks.verification_hint: Run check-report tests with a fixture that asks for a V3 surface while the manifest declares an MVP/V1 surface.

post_conditions.message: Check report must surface adapter compatibility blockers.
Check report tests must include adapter mismatch coverage.
Tests must assert stale adapter mismatch blocking.
Adapter check and evidence tests must pass.

tasks.title: Record provider spawn evidence
tasks.description: Add deterministic invocation evidence to provider spawn results: provider, command, args, cwd, stdin mode, timeout, output capture, budget, sandbox, preflight status, and terminal result.
acceptance_criteria: Provider spawn output includes invocation evidence even when preflight blocks, timeout fires, or stdout is empty.
The evidence uses stable field names for command, cwd, stdin, output, budget, and sandbox.
tasks.verification_hint: Provider adapter tests should use fake spawn and fake clocks, not real provider execution.

post_conditions.message: Provider adapter must record invocation evidence.
Provider adapter tests must cover invocation evidence.
Provider adapter must expose invocation metadata.
Provider adapter tests must pass.

tasks.title: Align provider dry-run matrix
tasks.description: Align provider runtime and CLI dry-run matrix output with the real provider invocation evidence contract without spawning provider CLIs.
acceptance_criteria: Dry-run matrix and real spawn evidence use compatible fields for command, cwd, stdin, output, budget, and sandbox.
Dry-run matrix still refuses real provider execution, credentials, and billable calls.
tasks.verification_hint: Run the provider-runtime-matrix focused suite; it must stay dry-run only.

post_conditions.message: Provider runtime matrix must align with invocation evidence.
Provider matrix tests must cover invocation evidence.
Provider matrix tests must assert output capture metadata.
Provider matrix tests must pass.

tasks.title: Record terminal session attempts
tasks.description: Ensure timeout, empty provider output, blocked preflight, and partial-output/no-diff sessions produce durable attempt evidence before retry or failure handling.
acceptance_criteria: Timed-out provider sessions record timeout evidence and do not remain in running state.
Attempt records include provider invocation evidence and terminal session metadata.
tasks.verification_hint: Use fake provider responses in focused session-attempt tests; do not spawn real providers.

post_conditions.message: Session attempt handling must change.
Session attempt tests must cover terminal provider attempts.
Session attempt tests must assert terminal attempt evidence.
Session attempt tests must pass.

tasks.title: Prevent manual takeover success
tasks.description: Classify provider terminal failures with bounded retry and no path for a later manual agent to be counted as the same run success.
acceptance_criteria: Empty-output or no-diff sessions are failed or blocked through the runner, not recovered by invisible manual agent work.
Retry count is bounded and terminal records include provider invocation evidence.
tasks.verification_hint: Use fake provider failures in focused outcome tests.

post_conditions.message: Session failure classification must change.
Session outcome tests must cover terminal provider failures.
Tests must explicitly prevent manual takeover from counting as run success.
Session failure outcome tests must pass.

tasks.title: Stabilize worktree dependencies
tasks.description: Make git and filesystem worktree modes dependency-stable by linking/copying node_modules when safe, or by recording an explicit dependency-install blocker/plan before gates run in a worktree missing package executables.
acceptance_criteria: Git worktrees retain dependency access through a deterministic link/copy path.
Filesystem fallback worktrees either get dependency access or record a blocker before running commands that require missing package binaries.
The fix does not copy .git, runtime state, or unrelated workspace noise.
tasks.verification_hint: Worktree-session tests should simulate missing node_modules and package binaries without running npm install for real.

post_conditions.message: Worktree dependency preparation must change.
Worktree tests must cover dependency preparation.
Tests must assert node_modules/dependency behavior.
Worktree session tests must pass.

tasks.title: Import controlled recovery checkpoints
tasks.description: Add an explicit controlled recovery evidence import checkpoint that verifies artifacts, target files, commands, timestamps, and post-condition results while preserving the original run failure status.
acceptance_criteria: Controlled recovery evidence has a separate schema/status from clean runner success.
Recovery checkpoints verify commands, artifacts, target files, and post-condition results before downstream gates can use them.
tasks.verification_hint: Focused lifecycle tests should create synthetic recovery evidence and assert both unlock and fail-closed cases.

post_conditions.message: Recovery checkpoint helpers must change.
Run lifecycle tests must cover recovery evidence import.
Lifecycle tests must assert controlled recovery evidence.
Recovery lifecycle tests must pass.

tasks.title: Report controlled recovery acceptance
tasks.description: Make acceptance reporting show the original run failure and the controlled recovery evidence path as separate facts before accept or ship can pass.

targets.description: Acceptance reporting of controlled recovery evidence

acceptance_criteria: Reports show original run failed or blocked, then recovered through an auditable path.
Acceptance remains blocked when controlled recovery evidence is missing or unverifiable.
tasks.verification_hint: Acceptance-report tests should assert both recovered and fail-closed cases.

post_conditions.message: Acceptance reporting must reflect controlled recovery.
Acceptance report tests must cover controlled recovery evidence.
Acceptance tests must assert controlled recovery evidence.
Acceptance report tests must pass.

tasks.title: Require fake-provider dogfood pack
tasks.description: Upgrade the real-project dogfood pack so public-use claims require a deterministic fake-provider lifecycle that runs init, discovery, plan, PRD, check, run, review, accept, and ship evidence without manual code takeover or billable provider execution.
acceptance_criteria: No-code-only dogfood is no longer enough for public-use or publishable claims.
The required dogfood lifecycle includes a deterministic fake provider run and explicit no-manual-takeover evidence.
The release gate still refuses publish, credential reads, and billable provider execution.
tasks.verification_hint: Run the P28-P32 and P37-P39 release tests; the fixture must remain deterministic and non-billable.

post_conditions.message: Real-project dogfood pack must require full fake-provider lifecycle evidence.
Real-project dogfood tests must cover full fake-provider lifecycle.
Dogfood pack must explicitly track manual takeover as disallowed evidence.
Release dogfood tests must pass.

tasks.title: Block public beta without dogfood
tasks.description: Wire the full fake-provider dogfood result into public beta evidence so release readiness blocks publishable claims when dogfood is no-code-only, missing runner evidence, or manually taken over.
acceptance_criteria: Public beta evidence blocks when full fake-provider dogfood evidence is absent or no-code-only.
The release gate still refuses publish, credential reads, and billable provider execution.
tasks.verification_hint: Run the P28-P32 release tests; public beta evidence must stay non-billable.

post_conditions.message: Public beta evidence gate must include full lifecycle dogfood readiness.
Public beta tests must cover the new dogfood blocker.
Public beta tests must block manual takeover evidence.
Public beta evidence tests must pass.
Release gate changes must build.
